name: Cleopatra
primary_text_notes: No writings by Cleopatra survive. The primary sources are exclusively external and largely hostile: Plutarch's Life of Antony (most detailed biographical narrative, written c. 75 CE from Augustan-era sources), Cassius Dio's Roman History Books 49-51 (senatorial perspective, openly hostile), Appian's Civil Wars (fragmentary but useful for political context), Strabo's Geography (who visited Egypt during the period and offers eyewitness geographic/economic context), and Josephus's Antiquities of the Jews (covers Cleopatra's Judean interactions, hostile but from a non-Roman angle). These collectively span the political, military, and economic dimensions of Cleopatra's reign c. 51-30 BCE. All are available in public-domain translations via LacusCurtius/Perseus/Wikisource. The downstream pipeline must treat every one of these as a hostile or at minimum externally-framed source and cross-reference with modern reconstructive scholarship (Schiff, Roller, Tyldesley, Ashton).
conference_context: World Beautiful Business Forum, Athens, May 2026. 12-voice AI Assembly deliberating overnight on conference sessions about more-than-human democracy, beautiful business, and the future of governance.
